title: Tube thoracostomy using an optical trocar
abstract: Systems and methods that can improve the safety and efficacy of chest tube thoracostomy are described. For example, this document provides systems and methods that facilitate direct visual confirmation of the proper placement of a chest tube within the thoracic space.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A chest tube thoracostomy system comprising: a chest tube, the chest tube defining a lumen therein; an optic probe comprising a shaft and a tip end, the optic probe configured for releasable engagement with the chest tube; and a viewing device configured for displaying images captured by the optic probe. 2. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 1, further comprising a light source, wherein light from the light source can be emitted from the tip end of the optic probe. 3. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 1, wherein the optic probe comprises fiber optics. 4. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 1, wherein the optic probe comprises a camera. 5. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 4, wherein the optic probe is configured to wirelessly transmit images captured by the camera to the viewing device. 6. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 1, wherein the optic probe comprises a lens disposed at the tip end. 7. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 1, wherein the tip end of the optic probe is controllably deflectable. 8. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 1, wherein the shaft of the optic probe is configured for releasable engagement with the lumen of the chest tube. 9. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 1, wherein the viewing system is battery operated such that the viewing system is cordlessly operable. 10. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 1, further comprising a sterile cover that is releasably coupleable on the optic probe. 11. The chest tube thoracostomy system of claim 10, wherein the sterile cover is configured as a single-use, disposable cover. 12. A method of installing a chest tube in a thoracic space of a patient, the method comprising: incising and dissecting a thorax of the patient to create an insertion pathway into the thoracic space; inserting a portion of the chest tube into the insertion pathway, wherein the chest tube is releasably engaged with an optic probe having a shaft and a tip end, and wherein the optic probe is coupled with a viewing device configured for displaying images captured at the tip end of the optic probe; advancing the chest tube and optic probe further into the thoracic space while viewing images, using the viewing device, of a thoracic region captured by the optic probe; and removing the optic probe from engagement with the chest tube while leaving the chest tube at least partially disposed within the thoracic region. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising attaching a proximal end portion of the chest tube to a vacuum source. 14. The method of claim 12, wherein the optic probe is coupled to a light source, and wherein light from the light source can be emitted from the tip end of the optic probe to illuminate the thoracic space. 15. The method of claim 12, wherein the optic probe comprises fiber optics. 16. The method of claim 12, wherein the optic probe comprises a camera. 17. The method of claim 12, wherein the optic probe comprises a lens disposed at the tip end. 18. The method of claim 12, wherein the tip end of the optic probe is controllably deflectable. 19. The method of claim 12, wherein the shaft of the optic probe is releasably engaged with a lumen of the chest tube. 20. The method of claim 12, further comprising transmitting to a remote image storage system, via a computer network in communication with the viewing device, the images of the thoracic region captured by the optic probe.
